verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, multiple independent studies using FACS, surface biotinylation, and immunogold EM confirm HSP60 protein body display on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in cancer cells (lymphoma, lung carcinoma, others), under stress (oxidative, heat, infection), and in inflammatory states. Surface localization is increased during tumorigenesis and infection-induced immune cell activation (macrophages, γδ T cells, B cells). This is a well-documented cancer-ecto/stress-ecto chaperone whose outer-leaflet pool is a legitimate therapeutic target; baseline is mitochondrial/intracellular.
